Perfect! Now I have all the pieces. Let me compile the comprehensive findings:

## Code Excerpts: Redux Toolkit Infinite Query Lifecycle

### 1. QueryLifecycle.ts - onQueryStarted Dispatch & queryFulfilled Resolution

**File:** `/home/ovistoica/Work/redux-toolkit/packages/toolkit/src/query/core/buildMiddleware/queryLifecycle.ts:441-506`

```typescript
// Lines 441-506
const handler: ApiMiddlewareInternalHandler = (action, mwApi) => {
  if (isPendingThunk(action)) {
    const {
      requestId,
      arg: { endpointName, originalArgs },
    } = action.meta
    const endpointDefinition = getEndpointDefinition(context, endpointName)
    const onQueryStarted = endpointDefinition?.onQueryStarted
    if (onQueryStarted) {
      const lifecycle = {} as CacheLifecycle
      const queryFulfilled =
        new (Promise as PromiseConstructorWithKnownReason)<
          { data: unknown; meta: unknown },
          QueryFulfilledRejectionReason<any>
        >((resolve, reject) => {
          lifecycle.resolve = resolve
          lifecycle.reject = reject
        })
      // prevent uncaught promise rejections from happening.
      // if the original promise is used in any way, that will create a new promise that will throw again
      queryFulfilled.catch(() => {})
      lifecycleMap[requestId] = lifecycle
      const selector = (api.endpoints[endpointName] as any).select(
        isAnyQueryDefinition(endpointDefinition) ? originalArgs : requestId,
      )

      const extra = mwApi.dispatch((_, __, extra) => extra)
      const lifecycleApi = {
        ...mwApi,
        getCacheEntry: () => selector(mwApi.getState()),
        requestId,
        extra,
        updateCachedData: (isAnyQueryDefinition(endpointDefinition)
          ? (updateRecipe: Recipe<any>) =>
              mwApi.dispatch(
                api.util.updateQueryData(
                  endpointName as never,
                  originalArgs as never,
                  updateRecipe,
                ),
              )
          : undefined) as any,
        queryFulfilled,
      }
      onQueryStarted(originalArgs, lifecycleApi as any)
    }
  } else if (isFullfilledThunk(action)) {
    const { requestId, baseQueryMeta } = action.meta
    lifecycleMap[requestId]?.resolve({
      data: action.payload,
      meta: baseQueryMeta,
    })
    delete lifecycleMap[requestId]
  } else if (isRejectedThunk(action)) {
    const { requestId, rejectedWithValue, baseQueryMeta } = action.meta
    lifecycleMap[requestId]?.reject({
      error: action.payload ?? action.error,
      isUnhandledError: !rejectedWithValue,
      meta: baseQueryMeta as any,
    })
    delete lifecycleMap[requestId]
  }
}
```

**Key Point:** `queryFulfilled` is a Promise that gets resolved at line 489 when the thunk fulfills. The resolve callback receives `{data: action.payload, meta: baseQueryMeta}`, where `action.payload` is the full accumulated `{pages, pageParams}` structure for infinite queries.

---

### 2. Infinite Query Refetch Loop - Sequential Page Fetching

**File:** `/home/ovistoica/Work/redux-toolkit/packages/toolkit/src/query/core/buildThunks.ts:680-765`

```typescript
// Lines 680-765
if (isQuery && 'infiniteQueryOptions' in endpointDefinition) {
  // This is an infinite query endpoint
  const { infiniteQueryOptions } = endpointDefinition

  // Runtime checks should guarantee this is a positive number if provided
  const { maxPages = Infinity } = infiniteQueryOptions

  // Priority: per-call override > endpoint config > default (true)
  const refetchCachedPages =
    (arg as InfiniteQueryThunkArg<any>).refetchCachedPages ??
    infiniteQueryOptions.refetchCachedPages ??
    true

  let result: QueryReturnValue

  // Start by looking up the existing InfiniteData value from state,
  // falling back to an empty value if it doesn't exist yet
  const blankData = { pages: [], pageParams: [] }
  const cachedData = selectors.selectQueryEntry(
    getState(),
    arg.queryCacheKey,
  )?.data as InfiniteData<unknown, unknown> | undefined

  // When the arg changes or the user forces a refetch,
  // we don't include the `direction` flag. This lets us distinguish
  // between actually refetching with a forced query, vs just fetching
  // the next page.
  const isForcedQueryNeedingRefetch = // arg.forceRefetch
    isForcedQuery(arg, getState()) &&
    !(arg as InfiniteQueryThunkArg<any>).direction
  const existingData = (
    isForcedQueryNeedingRefetch || !cachedData ? blankData : cachedData
  ) as InfiniteData<unknown, unknown>

  // If the thunk specified a direction and we do have at least one page,
  // fetch the next or previous page
  if ('direction' in arg && arg.direction && existingData.pages.length) {
    const previous = arg.direction === 'backward'
    const pageParamFn = previous ? getPreviousPageParam : getNextPageParam
    const param = pageParamFn(
      infiniteQueryOptions,
      existingData,
      arg.originalArgs,
    )

    result = await fetchPage(existingData, param, maxPages, previous)
  } else {
    // Otherwise, fetch the first page and then any remaining pages

    const { initialPageParam = infiniteQueryOptions.initialPageParam } =
      arg as InfiniteQueryThunkArg<any>

    // If we're doing a refetch, we should start from
    // the first page we have cached.
    // Otherwise, we should start from the initialPageParam
    const cachedPageParams = cachedData?.pageParams ?? []
    const firstPageParam = cachedPageParams[0] ?? initialPageParam
    const totalPages = cachedPageParams.length

    // Fetch first page
    result = await fetchPage(existingData, firstPageParam, maxPages)

    if (forceQueryFn) {
      // HACK `upsertQueryData` expects the user to pass in the `{pages, pageParams}` structure,
      // but `fetchPage` treats that as `pages[0]`. We have to manually un-nest it.
      result = {
        data: (result.data as InfiniteData<unknown, unknown>).pages[0],
      } as QueryReturnValue
    }

    if (refetchCachedPages) {
      // Fetch remaining pages
      for (let i = 1; i < totalPages; i++) {
        const param = getNextPageParam(
          infiniteQueryOptions,
          result.data as InfiniteData<unknown, unknown>,
          arg.originalArgs,
        )
        result = await fetchPage(
          result.data as InfiniteData<unknown, unknown>,
          param,
          maxPages,
        )
      }
    }
  }

  finalQueryReturnValue = result
}
```

**Critical Detail:** When `refetchCachedPages: true` (default), each call to `fetchPage()` at lines 740, 758-762 invokes `executeRequest()`, which is a **separate** thunk dispatch. This means for N pages, the main thunk dispatches N sub-requests, but **there is only ONE `onQueryStarted` call per main thunk**—not per page. Each page fetch is batched under the same `requestId`.

---

### 3. refetchCachedPages Type Definition & Branching Logic

**File:** `/home/ovistoica/Work/redux-toolkit/packages/toolkit/src/query/core/buildThunks.ts:157-167`

```typescript
export type InfiniteQueryThunkArg<
  D extends InfiniteQueryDefinition<any, any, any, any, any>,
> = QuerySubstateIdentifier &
  StartInfiniteQueryActionCreatorOptions<D> & {
    type: `query`
    originalArgs: unknown
    endpointName: string
    param: unknown
    direction?: InfiniteQueryDirection
    refetchCachedPages?: boolean
  }
```

**Branching Logic** (lines 687-691):
```typescript
const refetchCachedPages =
  (arg as InfiniteQueryThunkArg<any>).refetchCachedPages ??
  infiniteQueryOptions.refetchCachedPages ??
  true
```

**Options hierarchy:**
1. Per-call override via `endpoint.initiate({refetchCachedPages: boolean})`
2. Endpoint config via `infiniteQueryOptions: {refetchCachedPages: boolean}`
3. Default: `true` (always refetch all cached pages)

---

### 4. Test: "refetches all pages by default" (lines 1017-1089)

**File:** `/home/ovistoica/Work/redux-toolkit/packages/toolkit/src/query/tests/infiniteQueries.test.ts:1017-1089`

```typescript
test('refetches all pages by default (refetchCachedPages: true)', async () => {
  let hitCounter = 0

  const countersApi = createApi({
    baseQuery: fakeBaseQuery(),
    tagTypes: ['Counter'],
    endpoints: (build) => ({
      counters: build.infiniteQuery<HitCounter, string, number>({
        queryFn({ pageParam }) {
          hitCounter++
          return { data: { page: pageParam, hitCounter } }
        },
        infiniteQueryOptions: {
          initialPageParam: 0,
          getNextPageParam: (
            lastPage,
            allPages,
            lastPageParam,
            allPageParams,
          ) => lastPageParam + 1,
        },
        providesTags: ['Counter'],
      }),
    }),
  })

  const storeRef = setupApiStore(
    countersApi,
    { ...actionsReducer },
    {
      withoutTestLifecycles: true,
    },
  )

  // Load 3 pages
  await storeRef.store.dispatch(
    countersApi.endpoints.counters.initiate('item', {
      initialPageParam: 0,
    }),
  )

  await storeRef.store.dispatch(
    countersApi.endpoints.counters.initiate('item', {
      direction: 'forward',
    }),
  )

  const thirdPromise = storeRef.store.dispatch(
    countersApi.endpoints.counters.initiate('item', {
      direction: 'forward',
    }),
  )

  const thirdRes = await thirdPromise

  // Should have 3 pages with hitCounters 1, 2, 3
  expect(thirdRes.data!.pages).toEqual([
    { page: 0, hitCounter: 1 },
    { page: 1, hitCounter: 2 },
    { page: 2, hitCounter: 3 },
  ])

  // Refetch without specifying refetchCachedPages
  const refetchRes = await thirdPromise.refetch()

  // All 3 pages should be refetched (hitCounters 4, 5, 6)
  expect(refetchRes.data!.pages).toEqual([
    { page: 0, hitCounter: 4 },
    { page: 1, hitCounter: 5 },
    { page: 2, hitCounter: 6 },
  ])
  expect(refetchRes.data!.pages).toHaveLength(3)
})
```

**Assertion:** When `refetch()` is called, **all 3 pages are re-executed** (hitCounter increments from 1,2,3 to 4,5,6). This proves `refetchCachedPages: true` causes N network calls for N cached pages in a single refetch operation.

---

### 5. Lifecycle Test: Cache Methods Called (lines 857-941)

**File:** `/home/ovistoica/Work/redux-toolkit/packages/toolkit/src/query/tests/infiniteQueries.test.ts:857-941`

```typescript
test('Cache lifecycle methods are called', async () => {
  const cacheEntryAddedCallback = vi.fn()
  const queryStartedCallback = vi.fn()

  const pokemonApi = createApi({
    baseQuery: fetchBaseQuery({ baseUrl: 'https://pokeapi.co/api/v2/' }),
    endpoints: (build) => ({
      getInfinitePokemonWithLifecycles: build.infiniteQuery<
        Pokemon[],
        string,
        number
      >({
        infiniteQueryOptions: {
          initialPageParam: 0,
          getNextPageParam: (
            lastPage,
            allPages,
            lastPageParam,
            allPageParams,
          ) => lastPageParam + 1,
          getPreviousPageParam: (
            firstPage,
            allPages,
            firstPageParam,
            allPageParams,
          ) => {
            return firstPageParam > 0 ? firstPageParam - 1 : undefined
          },
        },
        query({ pageParam }) {
          return `https://example.com/listItems?page=${pageParam}`
        },
        async onCacheEntryAdded(arg, api) {
          const data = await api.cacheDataLoaded
          cacheEntryAddedCallback(arg, data)
        },
        async onQueryStarted(arg, api) {
          const data = await api.queryFulfilled
          queryStartedCallback(arg, data)
        },
      }),
    }),
  })

  const storeRef = setupApiStore(
    pokemonApi,
    { ...actionsReducer },
    {
      withoutTestLifecycles: true,
    },
  )

  const res1 = storeRef.store.dispatch(
    pokemonApi.endpoints.getInfinitePokemonWithLifecycles.initiate(
      'fire',
      {},
    ),
  )

  const entry1InitialLoad = await res1
  checkResultData(entry1InitialLoad, [[{ id: '0', name: 'Pokemon 0' }]])

  expect(cacheEntryAddedCallback).toHaveBeenCalledWith('fire', {
    data: {
      pages: [[{ id: '0', name: 'Pokemon 0' }]],
      pageParams: [0],
    },
    meta: expect.objectContaining({
      request: expect.anything(),
      response: expect.anything(),
    }),
  })

  expect(queryStartedCallback).toHaveBeenCalledWith('fire', {
    data: {
      pages: [[{ id: '0', name: 'Pokemon 0' }]],
      pageParams: [0],
    },
    meta: expect.objectContaining({
      request: expect.anything(),
      response: expect.anything(),
    }),
  })
})
```

**Key Assertion:** `onQueryStarted` fires **exactly once** per `.initiate()` call, receiving the full `{pages, pageParams}` structure. When the first page loads, `queryStartedCallback` is called with `pages: [[...]]` and `pageParams: [0]`—the complete accumulated state.

---

### 6. Direction & Fetch Mode Exposure in onQueryStarted

**File:** `/home/ovistoica/Work/redux-toolkit/packages/toolkit/src/query/core/apiState.ts:270`

```typescript
export type InfiniteQueryDirection = 'forward' | 'backward'
```

**File:** `/home/ovistoica/Work/redux-toolkit/packages/toolkit/src/query/core/buildThunks.ts:157-167`

```typescript
direction?: InfiniteQueryDirection
refetchCachedPages?: boolean
```

**Answer:** No, RTK Query **does not expose `direction`** to `onQueryStarted()`. The callback receives only `originalArgs` (the query arg, not the page param). The `direction` is internal to the thunk dispatch logic (line 709) and used to branch between `fetchNextPage`, `fetchPreviousPage`, and refetch. The hook does not receive "direction" or "mode"—it only receives the cache arg.

---

### 7. Docs: Refetch Behavior & onQueryStarted for Infinite Queries

**File:** `/home/ovistoica/Work/redux-toolkit/docs/rtk-query/usage/infinite-queries.mdx:64, 280-290`

From line 64:
> Infinite query endpoints are defined by returning an object inside the `endpoints` section of `createApi`, and defining the fields using the `build.infiniteQuery()` method. They are an extension of standard query endpoints - you can specify [the same options as standard queries](./queries.mdx#defining-query-endpoints) (providing either `query` or `queryFn`, customizing with `transformResponse`, lifecycles with `onCacheEntryAdded` and `onQueryStarted`, defining tags, etc).

From lines 280-290:
> When an infinite query endpoint is refetched (due to tag invalidation, polling, arg change configuration, or manual refetching), RTK Query's default behavior is **sequentially refetching _all_ pages currently in the cache**. This ensures that the client is always working with the latest data, and avoids stale cursors or duplicate records.
>
> There may be cases when you want a refetch to _only_ refetch the first page, and not any of the other pages in cache (ie, the refetch shrinks the cache from N pages to 1 page). This can be done via the `refetchCachedPages` option, which can be passed in several different places:
>
> - Defined on the endpoint as part of `infiniteQueryOptions`: applies to all attempted refetches
> - Passed as an option to a `useInfiniteQuery` hook: applies to all attempted refetches
> - Passed as an option to `endpoint.initiate()`, or the `refetch` method available on the `initiate` or hook result objects: applies only to the manually triggered refetch
>
> Overall, the refetch logic defaults to refetching all pages, but will override that with the option provided to the endpoint or a manual refetch.

---

## Summary

1. **onQueryStarted fires once per thunk dispatch**, not per page. For N cached pages being refetched, the same `onQueryStarted` callback is called exactly once, and `queryFulfilled` resolves with the **full `{pages: [...], pageParams: [...]}`** as the final cache state.

2. **Refetch loop is sequential**: The thunk awaits each `fetchPage()` call in order (lines 758-762). Each page is a separate `executeRequest()`, but they're all within the same thunk request cycle.

3. **refetchCachedPages defaults to `true`** and is hierarchical: per-call > endpoint > default.

4. **Direction is not exposed to onQueryStarted**—only the query arg is passed. The `direction` property is internal to thunk dispatch branching logic.

5. **Docs confirm**: "RTK Query's default behavior is **sequentially refetching _all_ pages currently in the cache**" when refetching.
````

## Agent (2026-04-24 15:57:38)

## Correction to my earlier summary

My earlier report about RTK Query was **wrong** on the key point. Let me correct the record:

##### TanStack Query (confirmed)
- **One callback per operation**, receives full accumulated `{pages, pageParams}`.
- `fetchNextPage` → one dispatch → do-while branch takes **single-page path** (lines 82-92 of `infiniteQueryBehavior.ts`) → one callback with the full merged `{pages, pageParams}`.
- `refetch` → one dispatch → do-while loops **all N pages** into one `result` → one callback at the end.
- `onSuccess(data, query)` — receives the full accumulated data plus the `Query` object; consumer reads `query.state.fetchMeta.fetchMore.direction` for `'forward' | 'backward'`.
- v5 removed `onSuccess`/`onError` from `useQuery` options entirely (kept them on Mutations). They live only on the global `QueryCache` config now.
- No per-page hooks. Direction is observable via `isFetchingNextPage` / `isFetchingPreviousPage`, both derived from `state.fetchMeta?.fetchMore?.direction`.

##### RTK Query (corrected)
- **One `onQueryStarted` per `.initiate()` call**, not N times. My earlier claim of N was wrong.
- Inside one `initiate` dispatch, the thunk sequentially awaits `fetchPage()` for all cached pages (buildThunks.ts:758-762), but it's all **one request** with one `requestId`. `queryFulfilled` resolves once with the final accumulated `{pages, pageParams}`.
- `refetchCachedPages: true` (default) is what drives the internal loop — it's a mode flag, not a callback multiplier.
- **Direction is not exposed** to `onQueryStarted`. The hook receives `(originalArgs, api)` where `api.queryFulfilled` is a promise of `{data, meta}`. Consumers can't tell `fetchNextPage` from `fetchPreviousPage` from within the hook.
- The lifecycle test at line 857 confirms: single `.initiate('fire', {})` call → `onQueryStarted` fires once → `queryFulfilled` resolves with `{pages: [[...]], pageParams: [0]}`.

#### The libraries actually agree

Both fire **one callback per user-intent operation** with the **full accumulated data**. They disagree only on whether direction is exposed:

- TanStack: yes, via `Query.state.fetchMeta` passed to the callback.
- RTK: no, direction is internal to the thunk.

#### Revised recommendation for re-frame-query

**Fire hooks once per operation, payload is always the full accumulated data, plus a `mode` arg.**

This means my earlier "payload varies by mode" proposal was wrong. Both industry implementations converged on a simpler model:

| Mode | `on-start` | `on-success` | `on-failure` |
|---|---|---|---|
| `:initial` | `params, :initial` | `params, :initial, {pages pageParams has-next? ...}` | `params, :initial, error` |
| `:append` | `params, :append` | `params, :append, {pages pageParams ...}` | `params, :append, error` |
| `:prepend` | `params, :prepend` | `params, :prepend, {pages ...}` | `params, :prepend, error` |
| `:refetch` | `params, :refetch` | `params, :refetch, {pages ...}` | `params, :refetch, error` |

**Why this is better than my earlier "payload varies" proposal:**

1. Consumers get a uniform handler signature; branching happens in user code via `case` on `mode` if needed — exactly what TanStack consumers do today.
2. Passing `mode` explicitly (vs. requiring consumers to read `fetchMeta`) is a cleaner API — RTK's decision to hide it is frequently cited as a pain point.
3. For `:append`/`:prepend`, users who only want the new page can `(last (:pages data))` — trivial and explicit.

This also gives us the "better than both" property: full data (like TanStack), plus explicit direction (unlike RTK).

Want me to proceed with this design?

118	    meta: BaseQueryMeta<BaseQuery>,
119	    arg: QueryArg,
120	  ): ResultType | Promise<ResultType>
121	  /**
122	   * A function to manipulate the data returned by a failed query or mutation.
123	   */
124	  transformErrorResponse?(
125	    baseQueryReturnValue: BaseQueryError<BaseQuery>,
126	    meta: BaseQueryMeta<BaseQuery>,
127	    arg: QueryArg,
128	  ): unknown
129	
130	  /**
131	   * A schema for the result *before* it's passed to `transformResponse`.
132	   *
133	   * @example
134	   * ```ts
135	   * // codeblock-meta no-transpile
136	   * import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query/react';
137	   * import * as v from 'valibot';
138	   *
139	   * const postSchema = v.object({ id: v.number(), name: v.string() });
140	   * type Post = v.InferOutput<typeof postSchema>;
141	   *
142	   * const api = createApi({
143	   *   baseQuery: fetchBaseQuery({ baseUrl: '/' }),
144	   *   endpoints: (build) => ({
145	   *     getPostName: build.query<Post, { id: number }>({
146	   *       query: ({ id }) => `/post/${id}`,
147	   *       rawResponseSchema: postSchema,
148	   *       transformResponse: (post) => post.name,
149	   *     }),
150	   *   }),
151	   * });
152	   * ```
153	   */
154	  rawResponseSchema?: StandardSchemaV1<RawResultType>
155	
156	  /**
157	   * A schema for the error object returned by the `query` or `queryFn`, *before* it's passed to `transformErrorResponse`.
158	   *
159	   * @example
160	   * ```ts
161	   * // codeblock-meta no-transpile
162	   * import { createApi } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query/react';
163	   * import { baseQueryErrorSchema, customBaseQuery } from './customBaseQuery';
164	   *
165	   * interface Post {
166	   *   id: number;
167	   *   name: string;
168	   * }
169	   *
170	   * const api = createApi({
171	   *   baseQuery: customBaseQuery,
172	   *   endpoints: (build) => ({
173	   *     getPost: build.query<Post, { id: number }>({
174	   *       query: ({ id }) => `/post/${id}`,
175	   *       rawErrorResponseSchema: baseQueryErrorSchema,
176	   *       transformErrorResponse: (error) => error.data,
177	   *     }),
178	   *   }),
179	   * });
180	   * ```
181	   */
182	  rawErrorResponseSchema?: StandardSchemaV1<BaseQueryError<BaseQuery>>
183	}
184	
185	export type EndpointDefinitionWithQueryFn<
186	  QueryArg,
187	  BaseQuery extends BaseQueryFn,
188	  ResultType,
189	> = {
190	  /**
191	   * Can be used in place of `query` as an inline function that bypasses `baseQuery` completely for the endpoint.
192	   *
193	   * @example
194	   * ```ts
195	   * // codeblock-meta title="Basic queryFn example"
196	   *
197	   * import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query/react';
198	   *
199	   * interface Post {
200	   *   id: number;

1	import { getEndpointDefinition } from '@internal/query/apiTypes'
2	import type {
3	  BaseQueryError,
4	  BaseQueryFn,
5	  BaseQueryMeta,
6	} from '../../baseQueryTypes'
7	import { isAnyQueryDefinition } from '../../endpointDefinitions'
8	import type { Recipe } from '../buildThunks'
9	import { isFulfilled, isPending, isRejected } from '../rtkImports'
10	import type {
11	  MutationBaseLifecycleApi,
12	  QueryBaseLifecycleApi,
13	} from './cacheLifecycle'
14	import type {
15	  ApiMiddlewareInternalHandler,
16	  InternalHandlerBuilder,
17	  PromiseConstructorWithKnownReason,
18	  PromiseWithKnownReason,
19	} from './types'
20	
21	export type ReferenceQueryLifecycle = never
22	
23	type QueryLifecyclePromises<ResultType, BaseQuery extends BaseQueryFn> = {
24	  /**
25	   * Promise that will resolve with the (transformed) query result.
26	   *
27	   * If the query fails, this promise will reject with the error.
28	   *
29	   * This allows you to `await` for the query to finish.
30	   *
31	   * If you don't interact with this promise, it will not throw.
32	   */
33	  queryFulfilled: PromiseWithKnownReason<
34	    {
35	      /**
36	       * The (transformed) query result.
37	       */
38	      data: ResultType
39	      /**
40	       * The `meta` returned by the `baseQuery`
41	       */
42	      meta: BaseQueryMeta<BaseQuery>
43	    },
44	    QueryFulfilledRejectionReason<BaseQuery>
45	  >
46	}
47	
48	type QueryFulfilledRejectionReason<BaseQuery extends BaseQueryFn> =
49	  | {
50	      error: BaseQueryError<BaseQuery>
51	      /**
52	       * If this is `false`, that means this error was returned from the `baseQuery` or `queryFn` in a controlled manner.
53	       */
54	      isUnhandledError: false
55	      /**
56	       * The `meta` returned by the `baseQuery`
57	       */
58	      meta: BaseQueryMeta<BaseQuery>
59	    }
60	  | {
61	      error: unknown
62	      meta?: undefined
63	      /**
64	       * If this is `true`, that means that this error is the result of `baseQueryFn`, `queryFn`, `transformResponse` or `transformErrorResponse` throwing an error instead of handling it properly.
65	       * There can not be made any assumption about the shape of `error`.
66	       */
67	      isUnhandledError: true
68	    }
69	
70	export type QueryLifecycleQueryExtraOptions<
71	  ResultType,
72	  QueryArg,
73	  BaseQuery extends BaseQueryFn,
74	  ReducerPath extends string = string,
75	> = {
76	  /**
77	   * A function that is called when the individual query is started. The function is called with a lifecycle api object containing properties such as `queryFulfilled`, allowing code to be run when a query is started, when it succeeds, and when it fails (i.e. throughout the lifecycle of an individual query/mutation call).
78	   *
79	   * Can be used to perform side-effects throughout the lifecycle of the query.
80	   *
81	   * @example
82	   * ```ts
83	   * import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query';
84	   * import { messageCreated } from './notificationsSlice';
85	   *
86	   * export interface Post {
87	   *   id: number;
88	   *   name: string;
89	   * }
90	   *
91	   * const api = createApi({
92	   *   baseQuery: fetchBaseQuery({
93	   *     baseUrl: '/',
94	   *   }),
95	   *   endpoints: (build) => ({
96	   *     getPost: build.query<Post, number>({
97	   *       query: (id) => `post/${id}`,
98	   *       async onQueryStarted(id, { dispatch, queryFulfilled }) {
99	   *         // `onStart` side-effect
100	   *         dispatch(messageCreated('Fetching posts...'));
101	   *         try {
102	   *           const { data } = await queryFulfilled;
103	   *           // `onSuccess` side-effect
104	   *           dispatch(messageCreated('Posts received!'));
105	   *         } catch (err) {
106	   *           // `onError` side-effect
107	   *           dispatch(messageCreated('Error fetching posts!'));
108	   *         }
109	   *       },
110	   *     }),
111	   *   }),
112	   * });
113	   * ```
114	   */
115	  onQueryStarted?(
116	    queryArgument: QueryArg,
117	    queryLifeCycleApi: QueryLifecycleApi<
118	      QueryArg,
119	      BaseQuery,
120	      ResultType,
121	      ReducerPath
122	    >,
123	  ): Promise<void> | void
124	}
125	
126	export type QueryLifecycleInfiniteQueryExtraOptions<
127	  ResultType,
128	  QueryArg,
129	  BaseQuery extends BaseQueryFn,
130	  ReducerPath extends string = string,
131	> = QueryLifecycleQueryExtraOptions<
132	  ResultType,
133	  QueryArg,
134	  BaseQuery,
135	  ReducerPath
136	>
137	
138	export type QueryLifecycleMutationExtraOptions<
139	  ResultType,
140	  QueryArg,
141	  BaseQuery extends BaseQueryFn,
142	  ReducerPath extends string = string,
143	> = {
144	  /**
145	   * A function that is called when the individual mutation is started. The function is called with a lifecycle api object containing properties such as `queryFulfilled`, allowing code to be run when a query is started, when it succeeds, and when it fails (i.e. throughout the lifecycle of an individual query/mutation call).
146	   *
147	   * Can be used for `optimistic updates`.
148	   *
149	   * @example
150	   *
151	   * ```ts
152	   * import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query'
153	   * export interface Post {
154	   *   id: number
155	   *   name: string
156	   * }
157	   *
158	   * const api = createApi({
159	   *   baseQuery: fetchBaseQuery({
160	   *     baseUrl: '/',
161	   *   }),
162	   *   tagTypes: ['Post'],
163	   *   endpoints: (build) => ({
164	   *     getPost: build.query<Post, number>({
165	   *       query: (id) => `post/${id}`,
166	   *       providesTags: ['Post'],
167	   *     }),
168	   *     updatePost: build.mutation<void, Pick<Post, 'id'> & Partial<Post>>({
169	   *       query: ({ id, ...patch }) => ({
170	   *         url: `post/${id}`,
171	   *         method: 'PATCH',
172	   *         body: patch,
173	   *       }),
174	   *       invalidatesTags: ['Post'],
175	   *       async onQueryStarted({ id, ...patch }, { dispatch, queryFulfilled }) {
176	   *         const patchResult = dispatch(
177	   *           api.util.updateQueryData('getPost', id, (draft) => {
178	   *             Object.assign(draft, patch)
179	   *           })
180	   *         )
181	   *         try {
182	   *           await queryFulfilled
183	   *         } catch {
184	   *           patchResult.undo()
185	   *         }
186	   *       },
187	   *     }),
188	   *   }),
189	   * })
190	   * ```
191	   */
192	  onQueryStarted?(
193	    queryArgument: QueryArg,
194	    mutationLifeCycleApi: MutationLifecycleApi<
195	      QueryArg,
196	      BaseQuery,
197	      ResultType,
198	      ReducerPath
199	    >,
200	  ): Promise<void> | void
201	}
202	
203	export interface QueryLifecycleApi<
204	  QueryArg,
205	  BaseQuery extends BaseQueryFn,
206	  ResultType,
207	  ReducerPath extends string = string,
208	> extends QueryBaseLifecycleApi<QueryArg, BaseQuery, ResultType, ReducerPath>,
209	    QueryLifecyclePromises<ResultType, BaseQuery> {}
210	
211	export type MutationLifecycleApi<
212	  QueryArg,
213	  BaseQuery extends BaseQueryFn,
214	  ResultType,
215	  ReducerPath extends string = string,
216	> = MutationBaseLifecycleApi<QueryArg, BaseQuery, ResultType, ReducerPath> &
217	  QueryLifecyclePromises<ResultType, BaseQuery>
218	
219	/**
220	 * Provides a way to define a strongly-typed version of
221	 * {@linkcode QueryLifecycleQueryExtraOptions.onQueryStarted | onQueryStarted}
222	 * for a specific query.
223	 *
224	 * @example
225	 * <caption>#### __Create and reuse a strongly-typed `onQueryStarted` function__</caption>
226	 *
227	 * ```ts
228	 * import type { TypedQueryOnQueryStarted } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query'
229	 * import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query'
230	 *
231	 * type Post = {
232	 *   id: number
233	 *   title: string
234	 *   userId: number
235	 * }
236	 *
237	 * type PostsApiResponse = {
238	 *   posts: Post[]
239	 *   total: number
240	 *   skip: number
241	 *   limit: number
242	 * }
243	 *
244	 * type QueryArgument = number | undefined
245	 *
246	 * type BaseQueryFunction = ReturnType<typeof fetchBaseQuery>
247	 *
248	 * const baseApiSlice = createApi({
249	 *   baseQuery: fetchBaseQuery({ baseUrl: 'https://dummyjson.com' }),
250	 *   reducerPath: 'postsApi',
251	 *   tagTypes: ['Posts'],
252	 *   endpoints: (build) => ({
253	 *     getPosts: build.query<PostsApiResponse, void>({
254	 *       query: () => `/posts`,
255	 *     }),
256	 *
257	 *     getPostById: build.query<Post, QueryArgument>({
258	 *       query: (postId) => `/posts/${postId}`,
259	 *     }),
260	 *   }),
261	 * })
262	 *
263	 * const updatePostOnFulfilled: TypedQueryOnQueryStarted<
264	 *   PostsApiResponse,
265	 *   QueryArgument,
266	 *   BaseQueryFunction,
267	 *   'postsApi'
268	 * > = async (queryArgument, { dispatch, queryFulfilled }) => {
269	 *   const result = await queryFulfilled
270	 *
271	 *   const { posts } = result.data
272	 *
273	 *   // Pre-fill the individual post entries with the results
274	 *   // from the list endpoint query
275	 *   dispatch(
276	 *     baseApiSlice.util.upsertQueryEntries(
277	 *       posts.map((post) => ({
278	 *         endpointName: 'getPostById',
279	 *         arg: post.id,
280	 *         value: post,
281	 *       })),
282	 *     ),
283	 *   )
284	 * }
285	 *
286	 * export const extendedApiSlice = baseApiSlice.injectEndpoints({
287	 *   endpoints: (build) => ({
288	 *     getPostsByUserId: build.query<PostsApiResponse, QueryArgument>({
289	 *       query: (userId) => `/posts/user/${userId}`,
290	 *
291	 *       onQueryStarted: updatePostOnFulfilled,
292	 *     }),
293	 *   }),
294	 * })
295	 * ```
296	 *
297	 * @template ResultType - The type of the result `data` returned by the query.
298	 * @template QueryArgumentType - The type of the argument passed into the query.
299	 * @template BaseQueryFunctionType - The type of the base query function being used.
300	 * @template ReducerPath - The type representing the `reducerPath` for the API slice.
```
````

### Tool Call [completed]: Read /home/ovistoica/Work/tanstack-query/examples/react/optimistic-updates-cache/src/pages/index.tsx

**Tool:** read

file_path: /home/ovistoica/Work/tanstack-query/examples/react/optimistic-updates-cache/src/pages/index.tsx

````
```
1	import * as React from 'react'
2	
3	import {
4	  QueryClient,
5	  QueryClientProvider,
6	  queryOptions,
7	  useMutation,
8	  useQuery,
9	  useQueryClient,
10	} from '@tanstack/react-query'
11	import { ReactQueryDevtools } from '@tanstack/react-query-devtools'
12	
13	const client = new QueryClient()
14	
15	type Todos = {
16	  items: ReadonlyArray<{
17	    id: string
18	    text: string
19	  }>
20	  ts: number
21	}
22	
23	async function fetchTodos({ signal }: { signal: AbortSignal }): Promise<Todos> {
24	  const response = await fetch('/api/data', { signal })
25	  return await response.json()
26	}
27	
28	const todoListOptions = queryOptions({
29	  queryKey: ['todos'],
30	  queryFn: fetchTodos,
31	})
32	
33	function Example() {
34	  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
35	  const [text, setText] = React.useState('')
36	  const { isFetching, ...queryInfo } = useQuery(todoListOptions)
37	
38	  const addTodoMutation = useMutation({
39	    mutationFn: async (newTodo: string) => {
40	      const response = await fetch('/api/data', {
41	        method: 'POST',
42	        body: JSON.stringify({ text: newTodo }),
43	        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
44	      })
45	      return await response.json()
46	    },
47	    // When mutate is called:
48	    onMutate: async (newTodo, context) => {
49	      setText('')
50	      // Cancel any outgoing refetch
51	      // (so they don't overwrite our optimistic update)
52	      await context.client.cancelQueries(todoListOptions)
53	
54	      // Snapshot the previous value
55	      const previousTodos = context.client.getQueryData(
56	        todoListOptions.queryKey,
57	      )
58	
59	      // Optimistically update to the new value
60	      if (previousTodos) {
61	        context.client.setQueryData(todoListOptions.queryKey, {
62	          ...previousTodos,
63	          items: [
64	            ...previousTodos.items,
65	            { id: Math.random().toString(), text: newTodo },
66	          ],
67	        })
68	      }
69	
70	      return { previousTodos }
71	    },
72	    // If the mutation fails,
73	    // use the result returned from onMutate to roll back
74	    onError: (err, variables, onMutateResult, context) => {
75	      if (onMutateResult?.previousTodos) {
76	        context.client.setQueryData<Todos>(
77	          ['todos'],
78	          onMutateResult.previousTodos,
79	        )
80	      }
81	    },
82	    // Always refetch after error or success:
83	    onSettled: (data, error, variables, onMutateResult, context) =>
84	      context.client.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] }),
85	  })
86	
87	  return (
88	    <div>
89	      <p>
90	        In this example, new items can be created using a mutation. The new item
91	        will be optimistically added to the list in hopes that the server
92	        accepts the item. If it does, the list is refetched with the true items
93	        from the list. Every now and then, the mutation may fail though. When
94	        that happens, the previous list of items is restored and the list is
95	        again refetched from the server.
96	      </p>
97	      <form
98	        onSubmit={(e) => {
99	          e.preventDefault()
100	          addTodoMutation.mutate(text)
101	        }}
102	      >
103	        <input
104	          type="text"
105	          onChange={(event) => setText(event.target.value)}
106	          value={text}
107	        />
108	        <button disabled={addTodoMutation.isPending}>Create</button>
109	      </form>
110	      <br />
111	      {queryInfo.isSuccess && (
112	        <>
113	          <div>
114	            {/* The type of queryInfo.data will be narrowed because we check for isSuccess first */}
115	            Updated At: {new Date(queryInfo.data.ts).toLocaleTimeString()}
116	          </div>
117	          <ul>
118	            {queryInfo.data.items.map((todo) => (
119	              <li key={todo.id}>{todo.text}</li>
120	            ))}
121	          </ul>
122	          {isFetching && <div>Updating in background...</div>}
123	        </>
124	      )}
125	      {queryInfo.isLoading && 'Loading'}
126	      {queryInfo.error instanceof Error && queryInfo.error.message}
127	    </div>
128	  )
129	}
130	
131	export default function App() {
132	  return (
133	    <QueryClientProvider client={client}>
134	      <Example />
135	      <ReactQueryDevtools initialIsOpen />
136	    </QueryClientProvider>
137	  )
138	}
139	
```
````

1	import * as React from 'react'
2	import {
3	  QueryClient,
4	  QueryClientProvider,
5	  useMutation,
6	  useQuery,
7	  useQueryClient,
8	} from '@tanstack/react-query'
9	import { ReactQueryDevtools } from '@tanstack/react-query-devtools'
10	
11	const client = new QueryClient()
12	
13	type Todos = {
14	  items: ReadonlyArray<{
15	    id: string
16	    text: string
17	  }>
18	  ts: number
19	}
20	
21	async function fetchTodos(): Promise<Todos> {
22	  const response = await fetch('/api/data')
23	  return await response.json()
24	}
25	
26	function useTodos() {
27	  return useQuery({ queryKey: ['todos'], queryFn: fetchTodos })
28	}
29	
30	function Example() {
31	  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
32	  const [text, setText] = React.useState('')
33	  const todoQuery = useTodos()
34	
35	  const addTodoMutation = useMutation({
36	    mutationFn: async (newTodo: string) => {
37	      const response = await fetch('/api/data', {
38	        method: 'POST',
39	        body: JSON.stringify({ text: newTodo }),
40	        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
41	      })
42	      if (!response.ok) {
43	        throw new Error('Something went wrong.')
44	      }
45	      return await response.json()
46	    },
47	    onSettled: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] }),
48	  })
49	
50	  return (
51	    <div>
52	      <p>
53	        In this example, new items can be created using a mutation. The new item
54	        will be optimistically added to the list in hopes that the server
55	        accepts the item. If it does, the list is refetched with the true items
56	        from the list. Every now and then, the mutation may fail though. When
57	        that happens, the previous list of items is restored and the list is
58	        again refetched from the server.
59	      </p>
60	      <form
61	        onSubmit={(e) => {
62	          e.preventDefault()
63	          setText('')
64	          addTodoMutation.mutate(text)
65	        }}
66	      >
67	        <input
68	          type="text"
69	          onChange={(event) => setText(event.target.value)}
70	          value={text}
71	        />
72	        <button disabled={addTodoMutation.isPending}>Create</button>
73	      </form>
74	      <br />
75	      {todoQuery.isSuccess && (
76	        <>
77	          <div>
78	            {/* The type of queryInfo.data will be narrowed because we check for isSuccess first */}
79	            Updated At: {new Date(todoQuery.data.ts).toLocaleTimeString()}
80	          </div>
81	          <ul>
82	            {todoQuery.data.items.map((todo) => (
83	              <li key={todo.id}>{todo.text}</li>
84	            ))}
85	            {addTodoMutation.isPending && (
86	              <li style={{ opacity: 0.5 }}>{addTodoMutation.variables}</li>
87	            )}
88	            {addTodoMutation.isError && (
89	              <li style={{ color: 'red' }}>
90	                {addTodoMutation.variables}
91	                <button
92	                  onClick={() =>
93	                    addTodoMutation.mutate(addTodoMutation.variables)
94	                  }
95	                >
96	                  Retry
97	                </button>
98	              </li>
99	            )}
100	          </ul>
101	          {todoQuery.isFetching && <div>Updating in background...</div>}
102	        </>
103	      )}
104	      {todoQuery.isPending && 'Loading'}
105	      {todoQuery.error instanceof Error && todoQuery.error.message}
106	    </div>
107	  )
108	}
109	
110	export default function App() {
111	  return (
112	    <QueryClientProvider client={client}>
113	      <Example />
114	      <ReactQueryDevtools initialIsOpen />
115	    </QueryClientProvider>
116	  )
117	}
118	
```

```
1	import React from 'react'
2	import Link from 'next/link'
3	import { useInView } from 'react-intersection-observer'
4	import {
5	  useInfiniteQuery,
6	  QueryClient,
7	  QueryClientProvider,
8	} from '@tanstack/react-query'
9	import { ReactQueryDevtools } from '@tanstack/react-query-devtools'
10	
11	const queryClient = new QueryClient()
12	
13	export default function App() {
14	  return (
15	    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
16	      <Example />
17	    </QueryClientProvider>
18	  )
19	}
20	
21	function Example() {
22	  const { ref, inView } = useInView()
23	
24	  const {
25	    status,
26	    data,
27	    error,
28	    isFetching,
29	    isFetchingNextPage,
30	    isFetchingPreviousPage,
31	    fetchNextPage,
32	    fetchPreviousPage,
33	    hasNextPage,
34	    hasPreviousPage,
35	  } = useInfiniteQuery({
36	    queryKey: ['projects'],
37	    queryFn: async ({
38	      pageParam,
39	    }): Promise<{
40	      data: Array<{ name: string; id: number }>
41	      previousId: number
42	      nextId: number
43	    }> => {
44	      const response = await fetch(`/api/projects?cursor=${pageParam}`)
45	      return await response.json()
46	    },
47	    initialPageParam: 0,
48	    getPreviousPageParam: (firstPage) => firstPage.previousId,
49	    getNextPageParam: (lastPage) => lastPage.nextId,
50	  })
51	
52	  React.useEffect(() => {
53	    if (inView && hasNextPage && !isFetchingNextPage) {
54	      fetchNextPage()
55	    }
56	  }, [inView, hasNextPage, isFetchingNextPage, fetchNextPage])
57	
58	  return (
59	    <div>
60	      <h1>Infinite Loading</h1>
61	      {status === 'pending' ? (
62	        <p>Loading...</p>
63	      ) : status === 'error' ? (
64	        <span>Error: {error.message}</span>
65	      ) : (
66	        <>
67	          <div>
68	            <button
69	              onClick={() => fetchPreviousPage()}
70	              disabled={!hasPreviousPage || isFetchingPreviousPage}
71	            >
72	              {isFetchingPreviousPage
73	                ? 'Loading more...'
74	                : hasPreviousPage
75	                  ? 'Load Older'
76	                  : 'Nothing more to load'}
77	            </button>
78	          </div>
79	          {data.pages.map((page) => (
80	            <React.Fragment key={page.nextId}>
81	              {page.data.map((project) => (
82	                <p
83	                  style={{
84	                    border: '1px solid gray',
85	                    borderRadius: '5px',
86	                    padding: '10rem 1rem',
87	                    background: `hsla(${project.id * 30}, 60%, 80%, 1)`,
88	                  }}
89	                  key={project.id}
90	                >
91	                  {project.name}
92	                </p>
93	              ))}
94	            </React.Fragment>
95	          ))}
96	          <div>
97	            <button
98	              ref={ref}
99	              onClick={() => fetchNextPage()}
100	              disabled={!hasNextPage || isFetchingNextPage}
101	            >
102	              {isFetchingNextPage
103	                ? 'Loading more...'
104	                : hasNextPage
105	                  ? 'Load Newer'
106	                  : 'Nothing more to load'}
107	            </button>
108	          </div>
109	          <div>
110	            {isFetching && !isFetchingNextPage
111	              ? 'Background Updating...'
112	              : null}
113	          </div>
114	        </>
115	      )}
116	      <hr />
117	      <Link href="/about">Go to another page</Link>
118	      <ReactQueryDevtools initialIsOpen />
119	    </div>
120	  )
121	}
122	
```
````

1	import { notifyManager } from './notifyManager'
2	import { Removable } from './removable'
3	import { createRetryer } from './retryer'
4	import type {
5	  DefaultError,
6	  MutationFunctionContext,
7	  MutationMeta,
8	  MutationOptions,
9	  MutationStatus,
10	} from './types'
11	import type { MutationCache } from './mutationCache'
12	import type { MutationObserver } from './mutationObserver'
13	import type { Retryer } from './retryer'
14	import type { QueryClient } from './queryClient'
15	
16	// TYPES
17	
18	interface MutationConfig<TData, TError, TVariables, TOnMutateResult> {
19	  client: QueryClient
20	  mutationId: number
21	  mutationCache: MutationCache
22	  options: MutationOptions<TData, TError, TVariables, TOnMutateResult>
23	  state?: MutationState<TData, TError, TVariables, TOnMutateResult>
24	}
25	
26	export interface MutationState<
27	  TData = unknown,
28	  TError = DefaultError,
29	  TVariables = unknown,
30	  TOnMutateResult = unknown,
31	> {
32	  context: TOnMutateResult | undefined
33	  data: TData | undefined
34	  error: TError | null
35	  failureCount: number
36	  failureReason: TError | null
37	  isPaused: boolean
38	  status: MutationStatus
39	  variables: TVariables | undefined
40	  submittedAt: number
41	}
42	
43	interface FailedAction<TError> {
44	  type: 'failed'
45	  failureCount: number
46	  error: TError | null
47	}
48	
49	interface PendingAction<TVariables, TOnMutateResult> {
50	  type: 'pending'
51	  isPaused: boolean
52	  variables?: TVariables
53	  context?: TOnMutateResult
54	}
55	
56	interface SuccessAction<TData> {
57	  type: 'success'
58	  data: TData
59	}
60	
61	interface ErrorAction<TError> {
62	  type: 'error'
63	  error: TError
64	}
65	
66	interface PauseAction {
67	  type: 'pause'
68	}
69	
70	interface ContinueAction {
71	  type: 'continue'
72	}
73	
74	export type Action<TData, TError, TVariables, TOnMutateResult> =
75	  | ContinueAction
76	  | ErrorAction<TError>
77	  | FailedAction<TError>
78	  | PendingAction<TVariables, TOnMutateResult>
79	  | PauseAction
80	  | SuccessAction<TData>
81	
82	// CLASS
83	
84	export class Mutation<
85	  TData = unknown,
86	  TError = DefaultError,
87	  TVariables = unknown,
88	  TOnMutateResult = unknown,
89	> extends Removable {
90	  state: MutationState<TData, TError, TVariables, TOnMutateResult>
91	  options!: MutationOptions<TData, TError, TVariables, TOnMutateResult>
92	  readonly mutationId: number
93	
94	  #client: QueryClient
95	  #observers: Array<
96	    MutationObserver<TData, TError, TVariables, TOnMutateResult>
97	  >
98	  #mutationCache: MutationCache
99	  #retryer?: Retryer<TData>
100	
101	  constructor(
102	    config: MutationConfig<TData, TError, TVariables, TOnMutateResult>,
103	  ) {
104	    super()
105	
106	    this.#client = config.client
107	    this.mutationId = config.mutationId
108	    this.#mutationCache = config.mutationCache
109	    this.#observers = []
110	    this.state = config.state || getDefaultState()
111	
112	    this.setOptions(config.options)
113	    this.scheduleGc()
114	  }
115	
116	  setOptions(
117	    options: MutationOptions<TData, TError, TVariables, TOnMutateResult>,
118	  ): void {
119	    this.options = options
120	
121	    this.updateGcTime(this.options.gcTime)
122	  }
123	
124	  get meta(): MutationMeta | undefined {
125	    return this.options.meta
126	  }
127	
128	  addObserver(observer: MutationObserver<any, any, any, any>): void {
129	    if (!this.#observers.includes(observer)) {
130	      this.#observers.push(observer)
131	
132	      // Stop the mutation from being garbage collected
133	      this.clearGcTimeout()
134	
135	      this.#mutationCache.notify({
136	        type: 'observerAdded',
137	        mutation: this,
138	        observer,
139	      })
140	    }
141	  }
142	
143	  removeObserver(observer: MutationObserver<any, any, any, any>): void {
144	    this.#observers = this.#observers.filter((x) => x !== observer)
145	
146	    this.scheduleGc()
147	
148	    this.#mutationCache.notify({
149	      type: 'observerRemoved',
150	      mutation: this,
```

12	export type NonUndefinedGuard<T> = T extends undefined ? never : T

14	export type DistributiveOmit<
15	  TObject,
16	  TKey extends keyof TObject,
17	> = TObject extends any ? Omit<TObject, TKey> : never
18	
19	export type OmitKeyof<
20	  TObject,
21	  TKey extends TStrictly extends 'safely'
22	    ?
23	        | keyof TObject
24	        | (string & Record<never, never>)
25	        | (number & Record<never, never>)
26	        | (symbol & Record<never, never>)
27	    : keyof TObject,
28	  TStrictly extends 'strictly' | 'safely' = 'strictly',
29	> = Omit<TObject, TKey>
30	
31	export type Override<TTargetA, TTargetB> = {
32	  [AKey in keyof TTargetA]: AKey extends keyof TTargetB
33	    ? TTargetB[AKey]
34	    : TTargetA[AKey]
35	}
36	
37	export type NoInfer<T> = [T][T extends any ? 0 : never]

40	  // defaultError: Error

47	export type DefaultError = Register extends {

49	}
50	  ? TError
51	  : Error
52	
53	export type QueryKey = Register extends {
54	  queryKey: infer TQueryKey

67	export const unsetMarker = Symbol('unsetMarker')

69	export type AnyDataTag = {
70	  [dataTagSymbol]: any
71	  [dataTagErrorSymbol]: any
72	}
73	export type DataTag<
74	  TType,
75	  TValue,
76	  TError = UnsetMarker,
77	> = TType extends AnyDataTag
78	  ? TType
79	  : TType & {
80	      [dataTagSymbol]: TValue

82	    }

86	    ? TaggedValue

88	
89	export type InferErrorFromTag<TError, TTaggedQueryKey extends QueryKey> =

93	      : TaggedError
94	    : TError
95	
96	export type QueryFunction<
97	  T = unknown,
98	  TQueryKey extends QueryKey = QueryKey,
99	  TPageParam = never,
100	> = (context: QueryFunctionContext<TQueryKey, TPageParam>) => T | Promise<T>

104	export type StaleTimeFunction<

110	  | StaleTime

113	export type Enabled<

122	export type QueryPersister<

125	  TPageParam = never,
126	> = [TPageParam] extends [never]

136	    ) => T | Promise<T>
137	
138	export type QueryFunctionContext<

```
1	import * as React from 'react'
2	import { useMutation, useQuery, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'
3	import * as api from './api'
4	
5	export const movieKeys = {
6	  all: () => ['movies'],
7	  list: () => [...movieKeys.all(), 'list'],
8	  details: () => [...movieKeys.all(), 'detail'],
9	  detail: (id: string) => [...movieKeys.details(), id],
10	}
11	
12	export const useMovie = (movieId: string) => {
13	  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
14	
15	  const movieQuery = useQuery({
16	    queryKey: movieKeys.detail(movieId),
17	    queryFn: () => api.fetchMovie(movieId),
18	  })
19	
20	  const [comment, setComment] = React.useState<string | undefined>()
21	
22	  const updateMovie = useMutation({
23	    mutationKey: movieKeys.detail(movieId),
24	    onMutate: async () => {
25	      await queryClient.cancelQueries({ queryKey: movieKeys.detail(movieId) })
26	      const previousData = queryClient.getQueryData(movieKeys.detail(movieId))
27	
28	      // remove local state so that server state is taken instead
29	      setComment(undefined)
30	
31	      queryClient.setQueryData(movieKeys.detail(movieId), {
32	        ...previousData,
33	        movie: {
34	          ...previousData.movie,
35	          comment,
36	        },
37	      })
38	
39	      return { previousData }
40	    },
41	    onError: (_, __, context) => {
42	      queryClient.setQueryData(movieKeys.detail(movieId), context.previousData)
43	    },
44	    onSettled: () => {
45	      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: movieKeys.detail(movieId) })
46	    },
47	  })
48	
49	  return {
50	    comment: comment ?? movieQuery.data?.movie.comment,
51	    setComment,
52	    updateMovie,
53	    movieQuery,
54	  }
55	}
56	
```
````

```
1	---
2	id: mutations
3	title: Mutations
4	sidebar_label: Mutations
5	hide_title: true
6	description: 'RTK Query > Usage > Mutations: sending updates to the server'
7	---
8	
9	&nbsp;
10	
11	# Mutations
12	
13	## Overview
14	
15	Mutations are used to send data updates to the server and apply the changes to the local cache. Mutations can also invalidate cached data and force re-fetches.
16	
17	## Defining Mutation Endpoints
18	
19	Mutation endpoints are defined by returning an object inside the `endpoints` section of `createApi`, and defining the fields using the `build.mutation()` method.
20	
21	Mutation endpoints should define either a `query` callback that constructs the URL (including any URL query params), or [a `queryFn` callback](./customizing-queries.mdx#customizing-queries-with-queryfn) that may do arbitrary async logic and return a result. The `query` callback may also return an object containing the URL, the HTTP method to use and a request body.
22	
23	If the `query` callback needs additional data to generate the URL, it should be written to take a single argument. If you need to pass in multiple parameters, pass them formatted as a single "options object".
24	
25	Mutation endpoints may also modify the response contents before the result is cached, define "tags" to identify cache invalidation, and provide cache entry lifecycle callbacks to run additional logic as cache entries are added and removed.
26	
27	When used with TypeScript, you should supply generics for the return type and the expected query argument: `build.mutation<ReturnType, ArgType>`. If there is no argument, use `void` for the arg type instead.
28	
29	```ts title="Example of all mutation endpoint options"
30	// file: types.ts noEmit
31	export interface Post {
32	  id: number
33	  name: string
34	}
35	
36	// file: api.ts
37	import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query'
38	import type { Post } from './types'
39	
40	const api = createApi({
41	  baseQuery: fetchBaseQuery({
42	    baseUrl: '/',
43	  }),
44	  tagTypes: ['Post'],
45	  endpoints: (build) => ({
46	    // The mutation accepts a `Partial<Post>` arg, and returns a `Post`
47	    updatePost: build.mutation<Post, Partial<Post> & Pick<Post, 'id'>>({
48	      // highlight-start
49	      // note: an optional `queryFn` may be used in place of `query`
50	      query: ({ id, ...patch }) => ({
51	        url: `post/${id}`,
52	        method: 'PATCH',
53	        body: patch,
54	      }),
55	      // Pick out data and prevent nested properties in a hook or selector
56	      transformResponse: (response: { data: Post }, meta, arg) => response.data,
57	      // Pick out errors and prevent nested properties in a hook or selector
58	      transformErrorResponse: (
59	        response: { status: string | number },
60	        meta,
61	        arg,
62	      ) => response.status,
63	      invalidatesTags: ['Post'],
64	      // onQueryStarted is useful for optimistic updates
65	      // The 2nd parameter is the destructured `MutationLifecycleApi`
66	      async onQueryStarted(
67	        arg,
68	        { dispatch, getState, queryFulfilled, requestId, extra, getCacheEntry },
69	      ) {},
70	      // The 2nd parameter is the destructured `MutationCacheLifecycleApi`
71	      async onCacheEntryAdded(
72	        arg,
73	        {
74	          dispatch,
75	          getState,
76	          extra,
77	          requestId,
78	          cacheEntryRemoved,
79	          cacheDataLoaded,
80	          getCacheEntry,
81	        },
82	      ) {},
83	      // highlight-end
84	    }),
85	  }),
86	})
87	```
88	
89	:::info
90	
91	The `onQueryStarted` method can be used for [optimistic updates](./manual-cache-updates.mdx#optimistic-updates)
92	
93	:::
94	
95	## Performing Mutations with React Hooks
96	
97	### Mutation Hook Behavior
98	
99	Unlike `useQuery`, `useMutation` returns a tuple. The first item in the tuple is the "trigger" function and the second element contains an object with `status`, `error`, and `data`.
100	
101	Unlike the `useQuery` hook, the `useMutation` hook doesn't execute automatically. To run a mutation you have to call the trigger function returned as the first tuple value from the hook.
102	
103	See [`useMutation`](../api/created-api/hooks.mdx#usemutation) for the hook signature and additional details.
104	
105	### Frequently Used Mutation Hook Return Values
106	
107	The `useMutation` hook returns a tuple containing a "mutation trigger" function, as well as an object containing properties about the "mutation result".
108	
109	The "mutation trigger" is a function that when called, will fire off the mutation request for that endpoint. Calling the "mutation trigger" returns a promise with an `unwrap` property, which can be called to unwrap the mutation call and provide the raw response/error. This can be useful if you wish to determine whether the mutation succeeds/fails inline at the call-site.
110	
111	The "mutation result" is an object containing properties such as the latest `data` for the mutation request, as well as status booleans for the current request lifecycle state.
112	
113	Below are some of the most frequently used properties on the "mutation result" object. Refer to [`useMutation`](../api/created-api/hooks.mdx#usemutation) for an extensive list of all returned properties.
114	
115	- `data` - The data returned from the latest trigger response, if present. If subsequent triggers from the same hook instance are called, this will return undefined until the new data is received. Consider component level caching if the previous response data is required for a smooth transition to new data.
116	- `error` - The error result if present.
117	- `isUninitialized` - When true, indicates that the mutation has not been fired yet.
118	- `isLoading` - When true, indicates that the mutation has been fired and is awaiting a response.
119	- `isSuccess` - When true, indicates that the last mutation fired has data from a successful request.
120	- `isError` - When true, indicates that the last mutation fired resulted in an error state.
121	- `reset` - A method to reset the hook back to its original state and remove the current result from the cache
122	
123	:::note
124	
125	With RTK Query, a mutation does not contain a semantic distinction between 'loading' and 'fetching' in the way that a [query does](./queries.mdx#frequently-used-query-hook-return-values). For a mutation, subsequent calls are not assumed to be necessarily related, so a mutation is either 'loading' or 'not loading', with no concept of 're-fetching'.
126	
127	:::
128	
129	### Shared Mutation Results
130	
131	By default, separate instances of a `useMutation` hook are not inherently related to each other.
132	Triggering one instance will not affect the result for a separate instance. This applies regardless
133	of whether the hooks are called within the same component, or different components.
134	
135	```tsx no-transpile
136	export const ComponentOne = () => {
137	  // Triggering `updatePostOne` will affect the result in this component,
138	  // but not the result in `ComponentTwo`, and vice-versa
139	  const [updatePost, result] = useUpdatePostMutation()
140	
141	  return <div>...</div>
142	}
143	
144	export const ComponentTwo = () => {
145	  const [updatePost, result] = useUpdatePostMutation()
146	
147	  return <div>...</div>
148	}
149	```
150	
151	RTK Query provides an option to share results across mutation hook instances using the
152	`fixedCacheKey` option.
153	Any `useMutation` hooks with the same `fixedCacheKey` string will share results between each other
154	when any of the trigger functions are called. This should be a unique string shared between each
155	mutation hook instance you wish to share results.
156	
157	```tsx no-transpile
158	export const ComponentOne = () => {
159	  // Triggering `updatePostOne` will affect the result in both this component,
160	  // but as well as the result in `ComponentTwo`, and vice-versa
161	  const [updatePost, result] = useUpdatePostMutation({
162	    fixedCacheKey: 'shared-update-post',
163	  })
164	
165	  return <div>...</div>
166	}
167	
168	export const ComponentTwo = () => {
169	  const [updatePost, result] = useUpdatePostMutation({
170	    fixedCacheKey: 'shared-update-post',
171	  })
172	
173	  return <div>...</div>
174	}
175	```
176	
177	:::note
178	
179	When using `fixedCacheKey`, the `originalArgs` property is not able to be shared and will always be `undefined`.
180	
181	:::
182	
183	### Standard Mutation Example
184	
185	This is a modified version of the complete example you can see at the bottom of the page to highlight the `updatePost` mutation. In this scenario, a post is fetched with `useQuery`, and then an `EditablePostName` component is rendered that allows us to edit the name of the post.
186	
187	```tsx no-transpile title="src/features/posts/PostDetail.tsx"
188	export const PostDetail = () => {
189	  const { id } = useParams<{ id: any }>()
190	
191	  const { data: post } = useGetPostQuery(id)
192	
193	  // highlight-start
194	  const [
195	    updatePost, // This is the mutation trigger
196	    { isLoading: isUpdating }, // This is the destructured mutation result
197	  ] = useUpdatePostMutation()
198	  // highlight-end
199	
200	  return (
201	    <Box p={4}>
202	      <EditablePostName
203	        name={post.name}
204	        onUpdate={(name) => {
205	          // If you want to immediately access the result of a mutation, you need to chain `.unwrap()`
206	          // if you actually want the payload or to catch the error.
207	          // Example: `updatePost().unwrap().then(fulfilled => console.log(fulfilled)).catch(rejected => console.error(rejected))
208	
209	          return (
210	            // highlight-start
211	            // Execute the trigger with the `id` and updated `name`
212	            updatePost({ id, name })
213	            // highlight-end
214	          )
215	        }}
216	        // highlight-start
217	        isLoading={isUpdating}
218	        // highlight-end
219	      />
220	    </Box>
221	  )
222	}
223	```
224	
225	## Advanced Mutations with Revalidation
226	
227	In the real world, it's very common that a developer would want to resync their local data cache with the server after performing a mutation (aka "revalidation"). RTK Query takes a more centralized approach to this and requires you to configure the invalidation behavior in your API service definition. See [Advanced Invalidation with abstract tag IDs](./automated-refetching#advanced-invalidation-with-abstract-tag-ids) for details on advanced invalidation handling with RTK Query.
228	
229	### Revalidation Example
230	
231	This is an example of a [CRUD service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Create,_read,_update_and_delete) for Posts. This implements the [Selectively invalidating lists](./automated-refetching#selectively-invalidating-lists) strategy and will most likely serve as a good foundation for real applications.
232	
233	```ts title="src/app/services/posts.ts"
234	// Or from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query' if not using the auto-generated hooks
235	import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query/react'
236	
237	export interface Post {
238	  id: number
239	  name: string
240	}
241	
242	type PostsResponse = Post[]
243	
244	export const postApi = createApi({
245	  reducerPath: 'postsApi',
246	  baseQuery: fetchBaseQuery({ baseUrl: '/' }),
247	  tagTypes: ['Posts'],
248	  endpoints: (build) => ({
249	    getPosts: build.query<PostsResponse, void>({
250	      query: () => 'posts',
251	      // Provides a list of `Posts` by `id`.
252	      // If any mutation is executed that `invalidate`s any of these tags, this query will re-run to be always up-to-date.
253	      // The `LIST` id is a "virtual id" we just made up to be able to invalidate this query specifically if a new `Posts` element was added.
254	      providesTags: (result) =>
255	        // is result available?
256	        result
257	          ? // successful query
258	            [
259	              ...result.map(({ id }) => ({ type: 'Posts', id }) as const),
260	              { type: 'Posts', id: 'LIST' },
261	            ]
262	          : // an error occurred, but we still want to refetch this query when `{ type: 'Posts', id: 'LIST' }` is invalidated
263	            [{ type: 'Posts', id: 'LIST' }],
264	    }),
265	    addPost: build.mutation<Post, Partial<Post>>({
266	      query(body) {
267	        return {
268	          url: `post`,
269	          method: 'POST',
270	          body,
271	        }
272	      },
273	      // Invalidates all Post-type queries providing the `LIST` id - after all, depending of the sort order,
274	      // that newly created post could show up in any lists.
275	      invalidatesTags: [{ type: 'Posts', id: 'LIST' }],
276	    }),
277	    getPost: build.query<Post, number>({
278	      query: (id) => `post/${id}`,
279	      providesTags: (result, error, id) => [{ type: 'Posts', id }],
280	    }),
281	    updatePost: build.mutation<Post, Partial<Post>>({
282	      query(data) {
283	        const { id, ...body } = data
284	        return {
285	          url: `post/${id}`,
286	          method: 'PUT',
287	          body,
288	        }
289	      },
290	      // Invalidates all queries that subscribe to this Post `id` only.
291	      // In this case, `getPost` will be re-run. `getPosts` *might*  rerun, if this id was under its results.
292	      invalidatesTags: (result, error, { id }) => [{ type: 'Posts', id }],
293	    }),
294	    deletePost: build.mutation<{ success: boolean; id: number }, number>({
295	      query(id) {
296	        return {
297	          url: `post/${id}`,
298	          method: 'DELETE',
299	        }
300	      },
301	      // Invalidates all queries that subscribe to this Post `id` only.
302	      invalidatesTags: (result, error, id) => [{ type: 'Posts', id }],
303	    }),
304	  }),
305	})
306	
307	export const {
308	  useGetPostsQuery,
309	  useAddPostMutation,
310	  useGetPostQuery,
311	  useUpdatePostMutation,
312	  useDeletePostMutation,
313	} = postApi
314	```
315	
316	<iframe
317	  src="https://codesandbox.io/embed/github/reduxjs/redux-toolkit/tree/master/examples/query/react/mutations?fontsize=14&runonclick=1&hidenavigation=1&module=%2Fsrc%2Fapp%2Fservices%2Fposts.ts&theme=dark"
318	  style={{
319	    width: '100%',
320	    height: '600px',
321	    border: 0,
322	    borderRadius: '4px',
323	    overflow: 'hidden',
324	  }}
325	  title="RTK Query - Mutations Example"
326	  allow="geolocation; microphone; camera; midi; vr; accelerometer; gyroscope; payment; ambient-light-sensor; encrypted-media; usb"
327	  sandbox="allow-modals allow-forms allow-popups allow-scripts allow-same-origin"
328	></iframe>
329	
330	## Runtime Validation using Schemas
331	
332	Endpoints can use any [Standard Schema](https://standardschema.dev/) compliant library for runtime validation of query args, responses, and errors. See [API reference](../api/createApi.mdx#schema-validation) for full list of available schemas.
333	
334	When used with TypeScript, schemas can also be used to [infer the type of that value instead of having to declare it](../usage-with-typescript.mdx#schema-validation).
335	
336	Most commonly, you'll want to use `responseSchema` to validate the response from the server (or `rawResponseSchema` when using `transformResponse`).
337	
338	```ts title="Using responseSchema" no-transpile
339	import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query/react'
340	import * as v from 'valibot'
341	
342	const postSchema = v.object({
343	  id: v.number(),
344	  name: v.string(),
345	  published_at: v.string(),
346	})
347	type Post = v.InferOutput<typeof postSchema>
348	const transformedPost = v.object({
349	  ...postSchema.entries,
350	  published_at: v.date(),
351	})
352	type TransformedPost = v.InferOutput<typeof transformedPost>
353	
354	const api = createApi({
355	  baseQuery: fetchBaseQuery({ baseUrl: '/' }),
356	  endpoints: (build) => ({
357	    updatePost: build.mutation<Post, Partial<Post>>({
358	      query(data) {
359	        const { id, ...body } = data
360	        return {
361	          url: `post/${id}`,
362	          method: 'PUT',
363	          body,
364	        }
365	      },
366	      responseSchema: postSchema,
367	    }),
368	    updatePostWithTransform: build.mutation<TransformedPost, Partial<Post>>({
369	      query(data) {
370	        const { id, ...body } = data
371	        return {
372	          url: `post/${id}`,
373	          method: 'PUT',
374	          body,
375	        }
376	      },
377	      rawResponseSchema: postSchema,
378	      transformResponse: (response) => ({
379	        ...response,
380	        published_at: new Date(response.published_at),
381	      }),
382	      // responseSchema can still be provided, to validate the transformed response
383	      responseSchema: transformedPost,
384	    }),
385	  }),
386	})
387	```
388	
```
````
